description_long: This is a modern, twin-engine civilian helicopter with a streamlined white fuselage accented by blue and yellow stripes. The helicopter features a large, rounded cockpit with expansive, dark-tinted windows for maximum visibility. Its main rotor has four long, slender blades with yellow tips, and the tail rotor is enclosed in a fenestron (ducted fan) configuration at the end of the tail boom, which is painted with a red, yellow, and green gradient. The landing gear consists of two sturdy, black skids with crossbars for stability. The helicopter's body is constructed primarily from lightweight metal alloys and composite materials, with visible seams and access panels. The overall design suggests it is intended for urban air mobility, emergency services, or police operations.
description: A sleek, white civilian helicopter with blue and yellow accents, large tinted cockpit windows, and a ducted tail rotor. It features four main rotor blades and black landing skids.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide cockpit, large tinted windows, and the symmetrical spread of the main rotor blades.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the blue and yellow stripes, the full length of the skids, and the fenestron tail rotor with colored markings.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail boom, fenestron tail rotor, and the symmetry of the main rotor blades.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the cockpit door, landing skids, and the colored tail fin with the fenestron rotor.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four long, narrow main rotor blades made of composite material, each with yellow tips and a slight upward curve, extending symmetrically from the rotor hub.
attribute_tail_rotor: Fenestron (ducted fan) tail rotor with multiple short blades enclosed in a circular housing at the end of the tail boom, featuring red, yellow, and green markings.
attribute_landing_skids: Two black, tubular landing skids with crossbars for stability, made of metal or composite, attached to the underside of the fuselage.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Large, curved cockpit windows wrapping around the front and sides, dark-tinted with black framing, providing wide visibility for pilots and passengers.
